objective: Implement dependency-free bounded XML 1.0 Fifth Edition legal-text and element-name value contracts from the accepted LSLC-001A public specification corpus, preserving accepted caller text exactly and returning indexed typed errors while explicitly deferring escaping, parsing, serialization, document assembly, LSL byte compatibility, protocol, and runtime behavior.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route bounded XML legal-text and element-name primitives while keeping representation, documents, LSL semantics, protocol, and runtime closed.
Describe only the implemented XML scalar/name validation contract and exact value preservation without claiming escaping, parsing, serialization, or LSL document compatibility.
Review whether the value-versus-representation boundary and historical-checker scoping add reusable contract guidance; keep repository-specific XML grammar detail out of the shared skill.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the clean Rusty LSL implementation and public-corpus boundary; retain the shared router unchanged if its existing public provenance rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Escaping, entity or character-reference selection, CDATA generation, parsing, serialization, byte output, document assembly, element ordering, attributes, namespaces, schemas, queries, canonicalization, or binding generic MetadataTree values to XML nodes.

LSL stream-info field mapping, exact endpoint bytes, empty-element form, whitespace, numeric spelling, channel-format wire spelling, oracle capture, candidate differential results, protocol-version behavior, or ecosystem compatibility.

Runtime identity, discovery, resolution, networking, sockets, inlets, outlets, transport, buffering, queues, threads, clocks, scheduling, recovery, providers, FFI, native loading, adapters, or language bindings.
Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, and production liblsl integration.

Morphospace adapters, Manifold admission or stream authority, topology, permissions, commands, platform lifecycle, Quest or Hostess behavior, device validation, and any parser, serializer, protocol, wire, runtime, performance, or official-endpoint compatibility claim.

acceptance.proof: A public validated text value uses an explicit nonzero Unicode-scalar maximum, accepts exactly the XML 1.0 Fifth Edition legal-character domain, preserves accepted text and allocation unchanged, and reports the first illegal scalar by zero-based scalar index and code point. Ampersand, less-than, and a caller value containing the CDATA close delimiter remain accepted values because representation policy is deferred.

A public validated element-name value applies the complete XML 1.0 Fifth Edition NameStartChar and NameChar ranges plus an explicit nonzero Unicode-scalar maximum, preserves accepted names exactly, and returns deterministic empty, one-past, invalid-start, and invalid-continuation errors with scalar indexes and code points. Colon remains syntax-only and grants no namespace semantics.

Focused tests cover every legal-character range boundary, representative prohibited controls and noncharacters, every NameStartChar/NameChar range boundary, continuation-only characters, Unicode scalar indexing, exact and one-past limits, empty names, exact value/allocation preservation, delimiters retained as caller text, and stable typed error precedence.

LSLC-001A remains specification-only with all oracle/candidate roles unobserved, historical STRM-000 and CORE overlays remain unchanged, the crate remains dependency/feature/unsafe-free, and historical focused checkers validate only their owned module surfaces rather than blocking later explicitly reviewed modules.

outputs: Dependency-free bounded XML legal-text and element-name accepted value, limit, accessor, and typed indexed-error APIs.
Positive and damaged Rust tests plus a separate LSLC-001B local-results overlay bound to the accepted corpus without changing its null oracle/candidate roles.

Deterministic LSLC-001B validation entrypoint, historically scoped checker repair, and public status updates that do not claim escaping, parsing, serialization, document, protocol, runtime, wire, or ecosystem support.
commit_policy: Commit the accepted XML name/text value contracts separately from escaping, parsing, serialization, document assembly, LSL field mapping, oracle capture, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, or runtime work.
